text:
Oh, I would love that story. Let me tell you one... Once upon a time in a vibrant city park nestled between gentle hills and blooming gardens, there lived a peculiar old cat.

Every day, after the sun had chased away the birds, this cat would make its way to a patch of grass, nestling between some pebbles and a shiny starfish. The cat wouldn't bathe—no, its fur was a shimmering slate gray. But every evening, the cat would stretch out, crack open a jar of pink mouse cookies, and purr until the stars emerged.

One summer night, as the moon hung high amidst a quilt of stars, the cat listened intently to a gentle wind whistle through the leaves. It began to chant,

"In the park I roam—
With mice to indulge my mood.
Past clouds that dare to play,
The stars I'll always stay."

Days turned to weeks, and the cat made new friends—from a wise old owl who
